name: Next-to-Minimal Supersymmetric Standard Model (NMSSM)
summary: Adds a gauge-singlet superfield S to the MSSM whose VEV dynamically generates the mu-term (Higgs-Higgsino mixing) via mu_eff = lambda <S>. Solves the MSSM mu-problem (why is the supersymmetric mu parameter at the EW scale, not Planck?), relaxes the LEP-tension upper bound on the Higgs mass at tree level (m_h up to ~ 130 GeV), and provides a richer Higgs sector including singlet-doublet mixing. The simplest viable extension of the MSSM.

addresses_puzzles.note: Builds on MSSM SUSY-protection of the Higgs mass while relaxing the LEP-imposed fine-tuning. Larger tree-level Higgs mass means less radiative-correction fine-tuning needed to reach 125 GeV; viable parameter space sits at lower fine-tuning than vanilla MSSM.
Singlet-doublet mixing pushes tree-level m_h up to ~130 GeV (vs MSSM bound m_h < 125 GeV at tree level); 125 GeV is easily accommodated with modest stop masses.
Singlino-dominated neutralino DM evades LZ bounds more easily than bino-dominated MSSM DM; viable parameter space remains for thermal-relic singlino.

touches_benchmarks.note: Singlet-doublet Higgs mixing produces O(few %) deviations from SM couplings; HL-LHC will pin down or exclude.
Singlino LSP has gauge couplings only through small mixing with binos/winos; SI cross-section suppressed below LZ.
Light singlino-Higgsino with light sleptons can fit (g-2)_mu in parts of parameter space, more easily than vanilla MSSM.

introduces.note: Builds on MSSM. New parameters: lambda, kappa (singlet self-coupling), A_lambda, A_kappa, mass <S>, scalar phase.

history: Originated with Fayet (1975), 'Supergauge invariant extension of the Higgs mechanism and a model for the electron and its neutrino', Nucl. Phys. B 90, 104 (singlet introduction). Modern NMSSM developed through 1990s-2000s. Comprehensive review: Ellwanger, Hugonie, Teixeira (2010), 'The next-to-minimal supersymmetric Standard Model', Phys. Rep. 496, 1, arXiv:0910.1785. Among the leading post-LHC SUSY candidates due to its relaxed Higgs-mass tuning.

references.citation: Ellwanger, Hugonie, Teixeira, 'The next-to-minimal supersymmetric Standard Model', Phys. Rep. 496, 1 (2010)
